steps: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/ and sign in with your Google account
Create a new project or select an existing one from the project dropdown
Navigate to 'APIs & Services' > 'Library' and search for 'YouTube Data API v3'
Click 'YouTube Data API v3' and then click 'Enable'
Go to 'APIs & Services' > 'OAuth consent screen' and configure it (select 'External' for personal use)
Click '+ Create Credentials' and select 'OAuth client ID'
Select 'Web application' as the application type
Add your redirect URI under 'Authorized redirect URIs' (e.g. https://yourapp.com/callback)
Click 'Create' and copy the Client ID and Client Secret

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client ID from Google Cloud Console

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client Secret from Google Cloud Console

notes: Free tier: 10,000 units per day quota (most reads cost 1 unit, video uploads cost 1,600)
YouTube Data API v3 does NOT support service account auth — user OAuth2 only
OAuth consent screen must be configured before creating credentials
Apps in 'Testing' status are limited to 100 test users; publish to production for broader access
To increase quota, submit a request through the Google Cloud Console

test_endpoint.description: Retrieve the authenticated user's YouTube channel info
